body:
Bead ddx-ffb678fc is an epic ("Story 5: artifact viewer categorization by metadata") whose sole acceptance criterion is "All child beads closed."

Decomposition already exists; no further decomposition or implementation work belongs at this level.

Children (all currently status=open):
  - ddx-345226c9 — artifacts: group-by control + grouping.ts + urlState.ts shared helper (Bead A; no deps)
  - ddx-31fba984 — artifacts: workflow-stage axis + paginated count wording + sessionStorage persistence (Bead B; depends on ddx-345226c9)
  - ddx-59459dd6 — artifacts: e2e for grouping + Story 6 composition check (Bead C; depends on A and B)

What is done in this attempt: nothing — the epic is a roll-up. Implementation lives in the child beads per the plan at /tmp/story-5-final.md.

What is blocking: the three child beads must each be executed and closed (in dependency order A → B → C). Until then the epic AC cannot be satisfied.

What a follow-up attempt would need: do not re-attempt this epic directly. Drain the child beads first (they are queueable as their dependencies clear). Once all three close, the epic AC is automatically satisfied and the epic can be closed without code changes.
